title: grove link silently skips directories that exist from git-tracked content
description:
LinkDirectoriesToWorktree (internal/workspace/link.go:49-51) checks os.Lstat(destPath) and skips if the path exists, regardless of why it exists.

When a linked directory (e.g., .beads) has force-tracked files inside it, git checkout populates that directory in new worktrees. Grove then sees the directory exists and silently skips creating the symlink â€” defeating the shared-state goal of [link].

Grove should detect when a link target exists due to git-tracked content and warn the user about the incompatibility.
design:
## Outcome

**Fix:** internal/workspace/link.go:48-57 distinguishes existing symlink (Skipped) from real dir (Conflicts). cmd/grove/commands/add.go:688-691 warns per conflict explaining the [link]/git-tracked incompatibility.
**Test:** internal/workspace/link_test.go "reports conflict when dest exists as real directory"; updated symlink-skip test.
**Evidence:** Real dir at dest now surfaces explicit warning instead of silent "already exists" skip.
**Status:** FIXED

title: grove add silently skips [link] patterns when run outside main worktree
description:
## Reproduction steps

1. `tau/main/.grove.toml` has `[link] patterns = [".beads", ".code-review-graph"]`.
2. From outside `tau/main`, run `grove add chore-changeset-release-flow`.
3. Inspect `tau/chore-changeset-release-flow/.beads`.

## Expected behavior

`chore-changeset-release-flow/.beads` is a symlink to `../main/.beads`, matching `[link]`. If linking cannot proceed, grove prints a warning or error.

## Actual behavior

No `.beads` symlink is created. No warning or error printed. A prior worktree `feat-tdd-flow` has the symlink, confirming the config works when invoked from the correct cwd.

## Root cause

- `cmd/grove/commands/add.go:651` calls `config.GetMergedLinkPatterns(sourceWorktree)` â€” reads `.grove.toml` from the resolved source worktree, not the main/default worktree.
- `findSourceWorktree` (`add.go:513`) uses cwd; outside a worktree it falls back to `findFallbackSourceWorktree` (`add.go:540`), which may return a worktree lacking `.grove.toml`.
- `internal/config/file.go:41` `LoadFromFile` returns an empty config on ENOENT without error.
- Result: patterns empty, link step skipped silently.

## Fix direction

Load `link`/`preserve` config from the main/default worktree rather than the resolved source worktree. Warn when the link step runs with zero patterns but a `.grove.toml` exists elsewhere in the repo.

## Acceptance

- `grove add <name>` applies `[link]` patterns regardless of cwd, as long as any worktree in the repo has `.grove.toml`.
- Regression test covers `grove add` from outside a worktree.
design:
## Outcome

**Fix:** Added `findConfigWorktree(bareDir)` in cmd/grove/commands/add.go to locate any worktree containing `.grove.toml`, preferring default branch then main/master by branch then dir name. `preserveFilesFromSource` and `linkDirectoriesFromSource` now take a `configWorktree` parameter used for `config.GetMerged*` lookups; data source remains `sourceWorktree`. All three runAdd call sites compute and pass it.
**Test:** `TestRunAdd_LinkPatternsAppliedFromOutsideWorktree` (cmd/grove/commands/add_test.go) runs `runAdd` from workspace root and asserts `.beads` symlink is created. Plus unit tests `TestFindConfigWorktree` and `TestLinkDirectoriesFromSource_LoadsConfigFromConfigWorktree`.
**Evidence:** End-to-end test exercises bug scenario (cwd outside any worktree) and passes only with the fix; full suite green; lint clean.
**Status:** FIXED
